title: symphytum sepulcrale subsp. sylvaticum
intro: Symphytum sepulcrale subsp. sylvaticum is a subspecies of Comfrey, a perennial herb native to Europe, known for its edible flowers and leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular treatment for wounds, burns, and other skin irritations. The medicinal properties of Symphytum sepulcrale subsp. sylvaticum are attributed to its high levels of allantoin, a compound that promotes tissue repair and reduces inflammation, leading to several health benefits including improved wound healing and reduced pain. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
